title: RuVector embedding and semantic database editing defined
simulation_note: This plan DEFINES the embedding, retrieval, branch, integrity, and merge contracts and the simulated COW/ATAS behavior. No embeddings were computed, no vectors written, no database rows created, no branch merged, and no source file overwritten. Blocked per CSV blocked_paths: live embeddings, database writes, source-file overwrite, autonomous merge, product implementation, secrets/**.
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
generated/user_space_ingestion_plan.json (POSTGRES-004 inputs; content hashes, AST parsing, module_path, checkpoints)

scope_boundaries.downstream_note: next_action decomposes retrieval, edit, branch, integrity, and merge into separate bounded target tasks after graph closure; this plan is the shared contract they inherit.

semantic_vector_knowledge_graph_class.sync_direction: embeddings computed by a local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model during the envctl bridge, then stored in PostgreSQL (redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL, forward-only)
semantic_vector_knowledge_graph_class.migration_path: RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation must be proven locally before this class fully cuts over; until then embeddings may be produced via the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path (NBVERIFY-004) and committed to PostgreSQL

durable_relational_macro_state_class.note: content-addressed code blocks / AST rows / causal edges are durable relational macro-state; redb may hold a transient forward buffer + the transitional durable blobs migrating via the BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover; envctl is the writer bridge

transient_high_frequency_class.note: BLAKE3 dedup cache, f32 geometry math, ESN ensemble scratch, and application-level WAL are transient; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class

per_agent_cognition_class.note: per-agent witness facts live in AgentDB but are witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors; non-authoritative for shared macro-state

derived_projection_class.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected by envctl / CodeDB materialization
derived_projection_class.note: source reconstruction / materialized directories are derived and disposable; re-projected deterministically from PostgreSQL via module_path

invariants: Single durable writer: every durable semantic-edit class (code blocks, embeddings, causal edges) is owned by PostgreSQL exactly once; redb, AgentDB, and ATAS timelines are never a second durable writer for these classes.

Database does not generate embeddings: PostgreSQL only stores and measures vectors; the local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model in the Rust/envctl binary produces them (Blueprint: 'the database itself does not generate embeddings; it only stores and measures them').
No-Sidecar: RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension; there is no separate vector database in the target (Blueprint §1 'No Sidecar'; Context Matrix row 26).

Content identity is authority: every code block is addressed by a SHA-256 content hash under the ADOPT-001 BlobStore contract; an edit produces a new content hash, never a silent in-place overwrite of identity.

Edits happen only in isolated COW branch rows: agents never UPDATE the main-trunk rows directly and never overwrite a source file on disk; the winning branch is committed, failing branches are rolled back.

Witness-before-commit: no edit reaches the main trunk without a SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector and a causal-graph edge with a historical root; hallucinated edits are rejected.

Deterministic merge: the winning timeline is committed by copying already-computed branch rows to the main trunk (the model is never asked to redo the work); selection is a deterministic function of ESN score + integrity gates.

Forward-only reproducibility: re-embedding a source block with the pinned model weights reproduces the identical vector, so semantic state is recoverable from source + model pin without trusting any single stored copy.

entity_authority_matrix.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR; content identity SHA-256; re-ingestible from source under the same hash

relationship.agentdb: none (blocks are shared macro-state, not per-agent cognition)
relationship.envctl: writer bridge that commits the block into PostgreSQL

entity_authority_matrix.entity: merge_decision (deterministic commit of the winning branch rows to main trunk)

entity_authority_matrix.planning_constraint: autonomous merge is BLOCKED in this planning task; the plan defines the gate, an executor task (decomposition target 'merge') performs a gated, non-autonomous commit later

relationship.agentdb: winning agent's witnessed edits are the merge payload
relationship.envctl: consumes the merged trunk later for projection

entity_authority_matrix.grounding: Blueprint §5 (Predictive Forecasting) 'it simply commits the successful database rows... eliminating the risk of the model failing to reproduce'; Context Matrix row 15

entity_authority_matrix.entity: rollback (failed-branch discard + PostgreSQL PITR + redb WAL)
entity_authority_matrix.write_authority: PostgreSQL discards branch rows / restores to a recovery point; redb buffers writes during PostgreSQL outage
entity_authority_matrix.sync_edge: branch fails -> discard branch (trunk untouched); PostgreSQL latency/outage -> redb WAL holds writes -> replay forward when PostgreSQL returns
entity_authority_matrix.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR; redb local disk (ACID) during the outage window

relationship.redb: application-level Write-Ahead Log during PostgreSQL latency/outage; forward-replayed
relationship.envctl: replays buffered redb WAL forward to PostgreSQL on recovery

entity_authority_matrix.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected by envctl
entity_authority_matrix.write_authority: envctl projection (read-only from PostgreSQL); never a reverse authority
entity_authority_matrix.sync_edge: PostgreSQL production branch -> module_path metadata maps directory -> concatenate raw_code blocks -> byte-exact .rs/.toml/.nu files -> build env
entity_authority_matrix.recovery_source: re-projection from PostgreSQL; independently, reproducible re-embedding restores semantic state

relationship.postgresql: source of truth for code rows and module_path metadata
relationship.codedb_nu_plugin: materialization can re-parse to verify round-trip
relationship.envctl: the projection engine (owned in detail by POSTGRES-008; used here for the reconstruction gate clause)

entity_authority_matrix.grounding: Blueprint §Materialization 'queries PostgreSQL... module_path metadata... concatenates raw_code blocks back into complete standard files'; ADOPT-001 exact-byte materialization; STORE-001 derived-projection class

retrieval.contract: target text -> pinned all-MiniLM-L6-v2 -> query vector; hybrid SQL: WHERE relational predicate (language, status) AND ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> [query_vector] LIMIT k, optionally BM25 keyword scoring and FastGRNN pre-filter. RuVector executes natively in PostgreSQL (No-Sidecar); redb only caches f32 distance math.

edit.contract: retrieved block -> UPDATE raw_code inside a COW branch row -> compute new SHA-256 content hash + re-embed + refresh causal edges. Never overwrites a source file; never touches main-trunk rows directly. Every edit carries a witness chain before it is eligible to merge.

branch.contract: clone main-trunk rows into isolated COW staging branches (MVCC isolation); agents work concurrently in separate branches; ATAS/ESN simulate parallel timelines (state diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, failure nodes) up to 100 steps and score each by stability (cosine similarity, strange-attractor bounds).

integrity.contract: RuVector MinCut continuously severs hallucinating agent clusters in O(n^{o(1)}); every claim/edit must present a SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector and pass Causal Graph Verification (a historical causal edge must exist) or it is rejected.

merge.contract: deterministic gate = (automated tests pass) AND (witness chain rooted) AND (MinCut/causal integrity intact) AND (ESN-selected winning timeline); merge commits the already-computed winning branch rows to the main trunk (model never re-runs). Failed branches roll back; PostgreSQL PITR + redb WAL provide recovery; envctl re-projects byte-exact source. Autonomous merge is blocked in planning; the executor performs a gated, non-autonomous commit.

gate_clause_coverage.model_and_vector_provenance: E03 embedding_model_pin (model id all-MiniLM-L6-v2, weights_sha256, dim=384, metric=cosine/<->, runtime Candle/ORT) bound to every E02 embedding; provenance is a PostgreSQL reference row, secrets stay in the keyring.
gate_clause_coverage.reproducible_embedding: E02+E03: re-embedding a source block with the pinned weights reproduces the identical vector (deterministic inference), which is exactly the recovery mechanism (PITR + re-embedding).
gate_clause_coverage.hybrid_search: E06: relational predicate + ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> + BM25 + FastGRNN, executed natively by RuVector in PostgreSQL (No-Sidecar); redb only caches f32 distance math.
gate_clause_coverage.branch_isolation: E07 COW staging branches (MVCC-isolated rows) + E11 MinCut isolation of hallucinating clusters.
gate_clause_coverage.concurrent_edits: E07+E08: each agent edits its own COW branch; MVCC prevents overwrite; conflicts resolved at deterministic merge, not by clobbering.
gate_clause_coverage.temporal_scoring: E09+E10: ATAS/ESN parallel timelines scored by stability (cosine similarity, strange-attractor bounds); RuvLTRA steers toward the most stable path.
gate_clause_coverage.witness_bound_decisions: E12 SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector + E13 causal-graph verification; unrooted/hallucinated edits rejected before merge.
gate_clause_coverage.deterministic_merge_gates: E14: gate = tests AND witness AND MinCut/causal integrity AND ESN selection; commit copies already-computed winning branch rows to trunk (model never re-runs).
gate_clause_coverage.rollback: E15: failed-branch discard + PostgreSQL PITR + redb application-level WAL during PostgreSQL latency/outage.
gate_clause_coverage.source_reconstruction: E16: envctl projection (production branch -> module_path -> byte-exact concatenation of raw_code blocks); independently, reproducible re-embedding restores semantic state.

open_dependencies: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL native extension activation is not yet locally proven; the semantic-vector class full cutover waits on it (STORE-001 open dependency; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder). Until then embeddings are produced via the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path (NBVERIFY-004) and committed to PostgreSQL.

POSTGRES-004 user-space ingestion contracts (content hashes, AST parsing, module_path, restartable checkpoints) are the upstream inputs this plan consumes; POSTGRES-002/POSTGRES-003 schema plans define the concrete code_block / branch / edge columns this plan references by role.
Deterministic merge and rollback here define the gate only; the actual gated commit is a later executor task (decomposition target 'merge'), never an autonomous merge in this policy-cell.
rollback_plan: Restore the prior semantic-edit plan; no vectors, branches, or source state are changed during planning.
